personal_info.full_name: Dinh Quang Minh Vu (preferred first name: Damian)
personal_info.current_title: Undergraduate Student Researcher, Full-Stack Developer, and Software Engineer Intern
personal_info.location: Gettysburg, PA (Open to Relocate and Remote)
personal_info.short_bio: Passionate about technology and math, I'm a junior student at Gettysburg College pursuing a Bachelor's in Computer Science and Math. I'm currently an undergraduate researcher assistant and a full-stack developer. I love using logical thinking to solve practical problems. That is why I love pure math and Algorithms a lot.

technical_skills.Languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, SQL, C++, HTML/CSS
technical_skills.Frameworks: React.js, Node.js, Express.js, Vue.js, SpringBoot, Docker, Kubernetes, Flask, Django
technical_skills.Tools: ZooKeeper, Kafka, RabbitMQ, Git, AWS, Amazon ElastiCache, Prometheus, Grafana, Postman, Figma, Vercel
technical_skills.ML/AI: PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face Transformers, Keras, FinBERT, OpenAI API, scikit-learn, NumPy

work_experience.key_achievements: Engineered 7 microservice APIs for Payment Authorization and Courier Dispatch using Java SpringBoot, decoupling workflows into scalable services. Implemented Apache Kafka and the SAGA pattern to ensure data consistency. Developed a Vue.js dashboard with PostgreSQL and the Outbox pattern to synchronize real-time order updates.

Developed and deployed a full-stack event management web app using React.js, Express.js, and AWS EC2 to handle scheduling and resource allocation. Managed 200+ student-event registrations using MySQL junction tables. Reduced event query response times by 80% by implementing an Amazon ElastiCache/Redis layer.

Engineered a scalable distributed system for synchronized AI security camera clusters, enabling real-time object recognition. Architected a leader election system using Apache ZooKeeper, ensuring 99.8% uptime with automated failover. Achieved 6% acceleration in object recognition processing by optimizing MongoDB schemas.

Co-authored 'Optimal Play of the All Yellow Zombie Dice Game', published in Springer Nature (CG 2024). Engineered a Python simulation library using NumPy to numerically solve scalar differential delay equations. Derived exact analytical solutions for complex delay differential equations.

Facilitated instructional support for Calculus II, Linear Algebra, and Object-Oriented Programming. Guided 60+ students through the implementation of core OOP principles and Java-based software development.

featured_projects.description: Architected an algorithmic trading microservices ecosystem processing 1000+ daily data points. Engineered dual predictive data pipelines, achieving 92% sentiment classification accuracy via FinBERT and 85% volatility forecasting via TensorFlow LSTM. Trained a PyTorch DQN reinforcement learning agent that outperformed baseline strategies by 14%.
featured_projects.tech_stack_used: Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, Hugging Face, FinBERT, OpenAI

featured_projects.description: Built a distributed, multi-microservice chat application in Go supporting large-scale marketing campaigns. Developed an Auth service with PostgreSQL/OAuth2.0 and integrated RabbitMQ to orchestrate asynchronous requests. Containerized and deployed services with Docker.
featured_projects.tech_stack_used: Go, Docker, Kubernetes, gRPC, Postgres, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, HTML, CSS

I am also open to freelance projects and collaborating on research projects that align with my interests: Chaos Discrete Dynamical Systems, Abstract Algebra and Number Theory, Distributed Systems, and ML.
